architecture_fit.note: Smallest delta from base_model.py: one attention block + one MLP; neighbour-counting genuinely done by attention via a toroidal relative-position bias.

baseline_comparison.note: Static copy baseline (F1 0.23-0.52) measured under identical boards plus global-attn and self-only ablations as strawmen, all failing where full circuit hits 1.0.

faithfulness.note: Causal ablations remove the neighbour mask (global attn collapses) and zero attention output (self-only -> 0 F1); README is honest it is hand-built.

operating_range.note: Grid-size sweep 8->64 (~64x cells) x density 0.1-0.5 all hold F1=1.0; exact circuit shows no breakdown across the range.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Every weight set by hand (Q/K=0, V reads alive bit, O scales to count, ReLU MLP encodes the exactly-3 rule) with no training.

visual_judgement.note: Five-panel board view with independent NumPy GT vs circuit, attention-pattern check, plus ablation and robustness tables make the claim legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-this-visualisation ties each panel to the claim: distinct code paths for the match map, attention panel proving 1/8 over 8 neighbours, ablation as falsifier.

notes: A transparent hand-built circuit that expresses one Game of Life step as a minimal base_model.py delta (one attention block + one MLP), with attention doing the neighbour-counting and a hand-set ReLU MLP applying the birth/survival rule. It scores a perfect F1=1.0 at every density and grid size, decisively clears the static baseline, and backs the claim with causal ablations and a clear independent-ground-truth visualisation. Every rubric item is fully satisfied.
